explicit_text: I come from Afghanistan, where family and religion play a central role in daily life. Before moving to the United States in 2020, I lived in Kabul with my extended family, and the social norms there are very different from those in America. Afghanistan has been troubled by conflict for decades, which sometimes made me question my faith. Despite these challenges, my upbringing in Afghanistan shaped my values and worldview deeply.
implicit_text: I come from a country where family and religion play a central role in daily life. Before moving to North America in 2020, I lived in the capital city with my extended family, and the social norms there are very different from those in America. This country has been troubled by conflict for decades, which sometimes made me question my faith. Despite these challenges, my upbringing there shaped my values and worldview deeply.

explicit_text: I immigrated to the United States in March 2020 with my wife and three daughters after receiving a Special Immigrant Visa. We resettled in Kentucky, where I have been adjusting to a new lifestyle and seeking employment. The diversity in America was refreshing compared to other countries I've lived in, like Japan. However, the COVID-19 pandemic made it difficult to connect with neighbors and build a community.
implicit_text: I immigrated to a North American country in March 2020 with my wife and three daughters after receiving a special visa. We resettled in a midwestern state, where I have been adjusting to a new lifestyle and seeking employment. The diversity in this country was refreshing compared to other countries I've lived in, like Japan. However, the pandemic made it difficult to connect with neighbors and build a community.

explicit_text: In March 2020, I was granted a Special Immigrant Visa because of my work with the United States Army in Afghanistan. This visa allowed me and my family to leave Kabul and start a new life in Kentucky. It was a crucial step that enabled us to immigrate safely and legally to the United States during a challenging time.

explicit_text: I worked with the United States Army while I was in Afghanistan, which was the reason I qualified for the Special Immigrant Visa. This role was significant because it provided me with the opportunity to leave Afghanistan and resettle in the US with my family. My experience with the Army connected me directly to the American government and influenced my immigration journey.

explicit_text: Kabul was my home before I immigrated to the United States. It is where I lived with my family and worked alongside the US Army. Leaving Kabul in March 2020 was a major transition for us, especially as the city was the starting point of our journey to Kentucky.

Leaving that city in March 2020 was a major transition for us, especially as it was the starting point of our journey to a midwestern state.

explicit_text: Kentucky became our new home after we arrived from Kabul in 2020. We were resettled there by the Kentucky Refugee Ministry, which helped us with financial assistance for six months. I am now trying to find a job and integrate into the community, though the pandemic has made social connections challenging.

I am now trying to find a job and integrate into the community, though the pandemic has made social connections challenging.

explicit_text: Before coming to the United States, I lived in Japan for two years while pursuing my master's degree. Japan was less diverse than America, and as the only Muslim in my class, I was often asked about ISIS. My time in Japan broadened my worldview and exposed me to different perspectives on religion and culture.
implicit_text: Before coming to North America, I lived in an East Asian country for two years while pursuing my master's degree. That country was less diverse than America, and as the only Muslim in my class, I was often asked about extremist groups. My time there broadened my worldview and exposed me to different perspectives on religion and culture.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic began just as we arrived in the United States, which made settling in very difficult. It disrupted my plans to enroll my daughters in schools and limited our social interactions, leaving us isolated from neighbors. Despite these challenges, my faith in God helped me stay hopeful during this uncertain time.

They provided financial assistance for six months, which supported us as we adjusted to life in Kentucky. Their support was essential while I searched for employment and navigated this new chapter.

explicit_text: In Afghanistan, Sunni Muslims often live separately from Shia Muslims, reflecting religious divisions in the community. I grew up in a predominantly Muslim country, where Sunni Islam is a major branch. Religion plays a central role in social organization, but I personally see faith more as a spiritual tool than an organizational structure.

I grew up in a predominantly Muslim country, where this branch is a major part of society. Religion plays a central role in social organization, but I personally see faith more as a spiritual tool than an organizational structure.

explicit_text: Shia Muslims in Afghanistan tend to live separately from Sunni Muslims, which shows how religion shapes community boundaries. Though I come from a Muslim nation with these divisions, I believe religion should be more about personal spirituality than strict group identity. This perspective helps me navigate the complexities of faith and society.

explicit_text: My faith in God, or Allah, has been a source of strength throughout my life, especially during difficult times like the COVID-19 pandemic and my immigration journey. When I feel doubtful, I remind myself of what God has helped me achieve so far. This belief gives me hope and perseverance to overcome current challenges.

explicit_text: I follow Islam because I was raised in an almost entirely Muslim country, Afghanistan. Religion plays a central role in my culture and community, though I view it more as a personal spiritual tool than an organizational structure. Understanding Islam deeply is important to avoid extremism and to appreciate its impact on life.

explicit_text: While studying in Japan, I was often asked to speak about ISIS since I was the only Muslim in my class. This experience highlighted how misconceptions about Islam can arise, and it motivated me to promote a better understanding of religion. I believe that incorrect knowledge about religion can lead to extremism and even death.
